title: phyllolepidum rupestre
intro: Phyllolepidum rupestre, also known as the "rocky tree fern", is a type of epiphytic fern native to tropical Asia, characterized by its thick, leathery fronds and ability to grow on tree trunks. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high content of saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Phyllolepidum rupestre include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying and processing its leaves into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
